description.description: Curated public compounds with known expected 3D molecular descriptor values. Used by the workbench feature_endpoints tests to validate the 3D descriptor pipeline (smiles-to-3d-full-v1). Expected ranges are intentionally wide — the purpose is to catch gross errors, not nitpick numeric accuracy.

columns.name: Compound common name
columns.npr1_min: Expected lower bound on npr1 (null = not asserted)
columns.axis_min: Expected lower bound on pharm3d_molecular_axis (Angstroms, heavy atoms)
columns.undefined_chiral_centers_expected: Expected value of the undefined_chiral_centers column produced by standardize() — count of chiral centers in the ORIGINAL input SMILES that have no stereo flag
columns.stereo_preserved_expected: Expected value of desc3d_stereo_preserved — True if the input's assigned stereo should survive 3D embedding (True for compounds with no chiral centers as well)
columns.notes: Why this compound is in the reference table

compounds.name: benzene
compounds.notes: Flat symmetric aromatic — textbook disc shape, no polar groups

compounds.name: naphthalene
compounds.notes: Planar bicyclic aromatic, elongated — off-corner disc

compounds.name: anthracene
compounds.notes: Three-ring linear acene — stretches NPR range further toward rod-like

compounds.name: decane
compounds.notes: Extended all-trans alkane chain — textbook rod shape

compounds.name: adamantane
compounds.notes: T_d-symmetric diamondoid cage — textbook sphere shape. RDKit's CIP detector flags the 4 bridgehead carbons as 'potentially chiral' but each is in 3 rings, so the ring-junction filter in standardize() excludes them. Net count is 0, which also reflects the chemistry (T_d symmetry means adamantane has no stereoisomers).

compounds.name: biphenyl
compounds.notes: Two aromatic rings with rotational freedom — twisted-disc torsion case

compounds.name: salicylic_acid
compounds.notes: Canonical IMHB former: ortho-OH donates to COOH carbonyl

compounds.name: 2_hydroxybenzaldehyde
compounds.notes: Canonical IMHB former: ortho-OH donates to aldehyde O

compounds.name: ethylenediamine
compounds.notes: H2N-CH2-CH2-NH2, 4-bond N-to-N — short aliphatic diamine

compounds.name: p_phenylenediamine
compounds.notes: Para-diamine on benzene — medium N-span (ring diameter + 2× C-N)

compounds.name: pyridine
compounds.notes: Aromatic N as pure H-bond acceptor — exercises hba_centroid_distance

compounds.name: nitrobenzene
compounds.notes: Regression gate: nitro N/O must NOT be counted as HBA. Expected pharm3d_hba_centroid_dist=0 (the nitro group is the only heteroatom and is excluded from the acceptor filter).

compounds.name: tetramethylammonium
compounds.notes: Regression gate: quaternary N+ must be counted as a charge site (formal_charge > 0 clause) but NOT as an HBA (no lone pair available). Td-symmetric — charge centroid coincides with COM.

compounds.name: morpholine
compounds.notes: Aliphatic heterocycle with both O acceptor and N-H donor in a ring

compounds.name: fluorobenzene
compounds.notes: Halogen + aromatic — exercises amphiphilic_moment C-F classification

compounds.name: l_alanine
compounds.notes: Smallest chiral reference — tests stereo preservation round-trip

compounds.name: s_ibuprofen
compounds.notes: Real drug with explicit stereo — tests stereo preservation on larger molecule

compounds.name: cis_stilbene
compounds.notes: E/Z bond stereo (complements sp3 stereo checks elsewhere)

compounds.name: ethanol
compounds.notes: Smallest drug-like molecule with a polar group

compounds.name: caffeine
compounds.notes: Rigid planar drug — general sanity check

compounds.name: aspirin
compounds.notes: Standard drug-like reference

compounds.name: testosterone
compounds.notes: Steroid SMILES with no stereo markers. The fused-ring scaffold has 6 potential stereocenters, but 5 are ring-junction atoms (in 2 rings each) and excluded by the ring-junction filter in standardize(); only C17 (bearing -OH, in a single ring) is counted. Exercises undefined_chiral_centers=1 reporting.
